channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FBI says 2 Homeland Security agents in Utah sold illegal drugs for profit through informant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Two Department of Homeland Security agents in Utah sold drugs that were previously seized as evidence through an informant in an illegal scheme that brought in up to $300,000, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: San Diego raises bar to work with immigration officials ahead of Trump's deportation efforts
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The nation's fifth most populous county decided Tuesday to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities beyond what California law dictates, allying itself with jurisdictions around the country that are raising new obstacles to President-elect Donald Trump's plans for mass
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What did you Google in 2024? From the elections to Copa América, here's what search trends show
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Remember what you searched for in 2024? Google does. Google released its annual “Year in Search” on Tuesday, rounding up the top trending queries entered into its namesake search engine in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: 2 bodies recovered amid debris from a fishing boat that reportedly capsized in Alaska
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Two bodies have been recovered amid debris from a fishing boat that reportedly capsized with five people aboard in rough seas in waters off southeast Alaska earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote
item.description: The House advanced the annual defense policy bill on Tuesday, teeing up the mammoth measure for a final vote later this week. The chamber voted 211-207 along party lines to adopt the rule — which governs debate for legislation — for the sprawling bill, formally opening up debate in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Timeline of UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting suspect's movements
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, was named as the suspect in the fatal shooting of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation

item.title: Man is charged with burning American flag during Washington protest over Netanyahu visit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal authorities have charged a man with burning an American flag in the nation’s capital during protests against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s address to Congress in July. Michael Snow Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DA suggests unusual idea for halting Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: Prosecutors are trying to preserve President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction as he returns to office, and they're suggesting various ways forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden to tout economic legacy in Tuesday speech
item.description: President Biden will deliver remarks Tuesday afternoon touting his economic agenda and making the case that it has been more effective than the kinds of tax cuts likely to be implemented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Details emerge about suspect charged with murder in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: ALTOONA, Pa. (AP) — The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO likely was motivated by his anger with what he called “parasitic” health insurance companies and a disdain for corporate greed, a law enforcement bulletin obtained by The Associated Press said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pelosi says she backs generational change on committees, but there are exceptions
item.description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) said this week that she supports the push for younger Democrats to replace veteran lawmakers at the top of committees — but there is at least one glaring exception to the rule.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Morgan Wallen alleged chair-throwing case to be bound over to grand jury
item.description: Morgan Wallen faces four charges related to his throwing a chair off a Broadway rooftop bar onto the street below in April.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: Slain UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson laid to rest in Minnesota
item.description: Police provided security at Monday's funeral service in Minnesota for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, who was fatally shot last week in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Video shows thieves speed off in sports cars from Michigan dealership
item.description: A group of suspected car thieves sped away from a dealership in rare sports vehicles, leading police on a high-speed chase in Caledonia Township.
item.dc:creator: Duncan MacLean, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, ABC 4

item.title: Party trays, meal kits recalled over cucumber-related Salmonella concerns
item.description: Multiple companies have issued recalls of products sold across the nation and in Utah after potential exposure to Salmonella through now-recalled cucumbers.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, NewsNation

item.title: FTC issuing refunds to Fortnite gamers after unwanted charges
item.description: According to the FTC, the company unlawfully charged players for unwanted purchases, let children rack up unauthorized charges without their parents’ permission, and blocked some users who disputed wrongful charges.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Altoona McDonald's slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: An Altoona McDonald's is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the murder of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Poet and literary celebrity Nikki Giovanni dies at 81
item.description: The poet, author, educator and public speaker who rose from borrowing money to release her first book to decades as a literary celebrity sharing her blunt and conversational takes on everything from racism and love to space travel and mortality, has died. She was 81.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Descendant of last native leader of Alaska island demands Japanese reparations for 1942 invasion
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Helena Pagano's great-grandfather was the last Alaska Native chief of a remote island in the Bering Sea, closer to Russia than North America.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 6 Guatemalans arrested and charged with human smuggling in deadly 2021 Mexico truck crash
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Six Guatemalans were arrested in Guatemala and in Texas Monday on human smuggling charges linked to a 2021 semitrailer truck crash in Mexico that killed more than 50 migrants, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey becomes latest state to prohibit bans on books in school, public libraries
item.description: PRINCETON, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy signed a law Monday to prohibit public and school libraries from banning books in the state and to enshrine protections against civil and criminal charges for librarians who comply with the law.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Smugglers escalate risky tactics as Trump second term looms
item.description: Sources say cartels are clearing out stash houses where migrants are piling up, moving people quickly ahead of Trump's mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Party trays, meal kits sold in Utah recalled over cucumber-related Salmonella concerns
item.description: Multiple companies have issued recalls of products sold across the nation and in Utah after potential exposure to Salmonella through now-recalled cucumbers.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VSP charged two for hit-and-run crash that killed a North Carolina woman
item.description: Virginia State Police are investigating a deadly multi-vehicle accident on I-81 in Wythe County that killed a North Carolina woman and seriously injured two others on November 30.
item.dc:creator: Thad Randazzo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge dismisses charges against Karen Read supporter who scattered rubber ducks and fake $100 bills
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Massachusetts judge dismissed criminal charges Monday against a backer of Karen Read who admitted placing dozens of yellow rubber ducks and fake $100 bills around town in support of Read. Richard Schiffer Jr.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal challenging Hawaii gun licensing requirements under Second Amendment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court rejected a challenge to Hawaii's gun-licensing law on Monday, though three justices expressed a willingness to hear arguments over the issue later. The majority did not explain their reasoning in a brief order declining to take the case.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Duck rescued from lake after getting tangled in fishing line, lure
item.description: Colorado deputies are asking people to correctly dispose of fishing lines and hooks after authorities waded through cold waters to rescue a tangled young bird.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: A pair of iconic ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen from a museum nearly two decades ago sold for a winning bid of $28 million at auction Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and HANNAH FINGERHUT Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Saints QB Derek Carr injures left hand on dive in 4th quarter of win over Giants
item.description: New Orleans Saints quarterback Derek Carr injured his left hand late in the fourth quarter of Sunday's 14-11 victory over the New York Giants when he went airborne while trying for a first down and crashed to the turf.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow, Russian state media say
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff's Office for sting operations between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
